title: Favorite scene from Eva 30
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Favorite scene from Eva 3.0             Quote Post by TheDrmcvey » Sun Feb 24, 2013 2:24 am Since this forum has seemed dead to me the last couple of days, I thought I should bring up a new topic for everyone to share their favorite scene from 3.0. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Feb 24, 2013 2:54 am Hard to whittle it down to even a few: -Wunder's take-off ('The Anthem'). -All of Shinji's psycho-drama moments. -Quatre Mains. -Shinji and Fuyutski playing Shogi, alongside all that exposition. -Descent into Terminal Dogma ('Return To Ash'). -Unit-02 trading blows with Eva-13. -Unit-02 verse the awakened Mark.09. -Fourth Impact; 'Scarred And Battled', 'From Beethoven 9' and 'The Wrath Of God...'. Basically all of it. -Cut of Shinji's face when he's realised what he's done. -The final red desert imagery where the first couple keys of 'Sakura Nagashi' start-up; Asuka, Shinji and Q Rei walking off into the horizon. Cue credits.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Feb 24, 2013 3:05 am Torn between the first 6 minutes and the final scene, after the battle. Given that what follows is corrupting my opinion of the first 6 minutes I'm going with the final scene, that one still has hope that it won't result in pain and suffering.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Feb 24, 2013 1:31 pm Definitely this scene, because it was creepy enough to make me shiver. And the music... yes, the music. Unbelievable.

[]:             Quote Post by TheDrmcvey » Sun Feb 24, 2013 1:31 pm Yeah that final scene is too good to pass up.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Feb 24, 2013 2:53 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Definitely this scene, because it was creepy enough to make me shiver. And the music... yes, the music. Unbelievable.

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Sun Feb 24, 2013 3:50 pm Favorites: -Unit-09 retrieves Shinji from the Wunder -Piano Scene -Scene where Kaworu removes the choker (that scene made tears well up), Shinji looked so happy for once. -Scene where Kaworu's head goes Boom, Ogata did an amazing performance in that scene. -Ending scene where the trio walk off into the red distance (one of the best cliffhangers I have ever scene).

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sun Feb 24, 2013 3:55 pm I loved 3.0 as a whole, so i'm mentioning scenes that i DIDN'T like: - The scene where Shinji is in his room at NERV HQ for the first time to scene right before Kaworu takes Shinji to see what happened to Tokyo-3. (These scenes were too slow and had little dialogue, but that's a minor complain. Though, i loved Shinji/Kaworu+piano moments.) So, i didn't like about 10-15 minutes of it, but this film is nonetheless "near perfection film."

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI_01 » Sun Feb 24, 2013 3:57 pm It's really hard for me to pick any scenes out... I really enjoyed 3.0 as a whole.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Feb 24, 2013 9:46 pm Hm, I don't think I'm ready to pick a favorite scene, it's the same with judging wether the movie was good or bad. Don't think i've processed/understood it enough, and it all hurts to much, I'm sorta "sterelized" to a degree. I feel like I would be talking about something I don't have a clue about, and only idiots do that. or maybe I'm not yet capable to judge it as art/quality from a viewer's perspective, I get any liking in an artistic manner mixed up with my pain about the in-universe events. Nothing good happens in this movie, nothing can really be "liked", it's the story of one fragile, nice, normal human being and the total collapse/disintegration/uraveling of all he is. I cannot even find the beauty of things falling apart in it, this is how shocked I am. I could always see the beauty in EoE, for example. From an ordinary school boy who seemed to have finally found himself after a long hard journey to an empty shell that won't even react to being kicked, alive only in the most crude physical sense that his heart is still pumping blood. I don't like anything about that, or any of the horrible things that happened to the other characters or the rest of the world, and yet I understand that this is completely independent from liking this movie as a movie , but I can't see it from that distance yet. - I could easily name the scenes that got the strongest reactions out of me (both mayor scenes in ReiQs chamber, the extreme closeup of Shinji's iris, SDAT-player-throw, and after a bit of hindsight, Mari stopping the impact and how she treats Shinji there/spares his life, and my shock by the sheer brutality of the last scene/Asuka's kick.) but to say which were good/which I liked? I can't say that yet. But I can try... always had a strange relationship with that expression after he realizes that he's talking to a fresh clone. There's so many things in that one reaction shot - realization, helplessness, the sensation of things slipping right through your fingers to your fingers that you can't change anymore, anger, (mostly at himself), and that single tear I only noticed at second glance. It's so, so complex... and so, so painful. --- One of the reasons why the final scene got so many different interpretations may be the starkness of it. There's no BGM and only pale daylight, like a documentary, in a way, it doesn't tell you what to think of it.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Feb 24, 2013 9:49 pm @Kendrix: Pish posh! You are not judging or analyzing the movie, just pick your favorite scene based on animation, action, music or anything else, not meaning...

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Feb 24, 2013 10:54 pm When Fourth Impact starts with Ode To Joy playing in the background. It was SO cash.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sun Feb 24, 2013 11:07 pm Mark.09 crashing through to retrieve Shinji is just one of my many favorite scenes in the film. I especially like the track they used for it, I believe it's called "Out from the Dark". Oh, any almost any scene with Misato, the AAA Wunder, and the bridge bunnies.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Feb 25, 2013 10:21 am TheFriskyIan wrote: When Fourth Impact starts with Ode To Joy playing in the background. It was SO cash.

[]:             Quote Post by alexyoshi » Mon Feb 25, 2013 10:46 am Some scenes that marked me by strong emotion they emit -> The scene where Shinji gets a new shirt that is too large, and we see the label with the name of Toji -> the death of Kaworu It's not as if I loved seeing Kawora die, but this scene was so intense, either at the staging and music

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Mon Feb 25, 2013 10:52 am alexyoshi wrote: -> The scene where Shinji gets a new shirt that is too large, and we see the label with the name of Toji -> the death of Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Feb 25, 2013 12:56 pm alexyoshi wrote: The scene where Shinji gets a new shirt that is too large, and we see the label with the name of Toji

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Wed Feb 27, 2013 8:02 am Another pick; Shinji walking around/exploring the ruins of Nerv HQ, looking for Rei. From memory, I think the piano 'L'Apôtre De La Lune' piece plays to it, and rather hauntingly.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Feb 27, 2013 8:34 am Too many moments - can't choose.

[]:             Quote Post by ScottAsher » Fri Mar 01, 2013 12:12 pm Right after Kaworu's head explodes and Mari has to stop the final impact from completing. The line she says was fucking amazing. Something like "Come on, give the princess some help puppyboy! And do me a favor, give the world a chance once in a while!" I don't even remember it exactly, all I know is I got chills. It was executed so perfectly.
